title: Besseya rubra
intro: Besseya rubra, also known as the crimson tufted globemallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae) native to the western United States. Traditionally, Besseya rubra has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Besseya rubra include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties. Besseya rubra is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in dried root forms for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Besseya rubra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as the plantain or snakeroot family. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Besseya rubra is a small perennial plant native to North America and is a member of the tribe Veroniceae within the family Plantaginaceae. The exact classification of Besseya rubra has undergone revision in recent years, but it is generally accepted as a distinct species within the family Plantaginaceae. Its classification is an ongoing area of research and study in the field of botany.
